svg.text: FRCS Part 1 (General Surgery) · UK practice (NICE CG141 · BSG · Baveno VII) · variceal vs non-variceal
retching → GOJ mucosal tear; often self-limiting
submucosal artery; brisk, hard to locate
prior aortic graft / AAA — herald bleed, can’t-miss
ABCDE; protect airway if haematemesis, ↓GCS or variceal bleed; high-flow O₂; two large-bore cannulae (14–16G)
FBC, U&E (urea ↑ out of proportion to creatinine), LFTs, clotting + fibrinogen, VBG + lactate, group & save + crossmatch
balanced crystalloid to restore perfusion; restrictive transfusion — transfuse if Hb <70 g/L, target 70–90; activate massive
platelets if <50 ×10⁹/L and actively bleeding (not if stable / not bleeding); warfarin → PCC + IV vitamin K; DOAC →
idarucizumab (dabigatran) / andexanet alfa (apixaban, rivaroxaban); avoid rFVIIa unless all else fails
urinary catheter, HDU / ICU if unstable; keep nil-by-mouth for endoscopy
urea, Hb, systolic BP, pulse ≥100, melaena, syncope,
hepatic disease, cardiac failure — no endoscopic data
first assessment of all patients; GBS 0 → consider early
adds endoscopic diagnosis + stigmata of recent
rebleeding and mortality; NICE: full Rockall after
AIMS65 (albumin, INR, mental state, SBP, age) → mortality
severe active bleeding → OGD immediately after resuscitation
suspected variceal bleeding → within 12 h (Baveno VII)
do NOT give PPI before endoscopy (NICE) — give afterwards only if high-risk stigmata; a prokinetic (erythromycin) can improve
FORREST CLASSIFICATION — stigmata of recent haemorrhage & rebleed risk
*natural-history rebleed estimates without treatment (Laine & Peterson, NEJM 1994); classification: Forrest, Lancet 1974.
DUAL therapy — adrenaline injection PLUS a second modality (mechanical clip / thermal coagulation / haemostatic powder).
high-dose IV PPI AFTER endoscopy for high-risk stigmata (Ia / Ib / IIa; IIb after clot removal) — e.g. 72 h infusion,
repeat endoscopy first-line; if it fails → interventional radiology (transcatheter arterial embolisation); surgery if IR
test & treat H. pylori; stop NSAIDs; continue low-dose aspirin once haemostasis achieved (NICE); complete PPI course.
cirrhosis / portal HT — treat empirically if suspected
terlipressin ASAP (splanchnic vasoconstrictor; continue 2–5 days) + prophylactic antibiotics from admission (e.g. IV
ceftriaxone) — both reduce mortality (NICE / Baveno VII)
oesophageal varices → endoscopic band ligation (EVL); gastric fundal varices (IGV1 / GOV2) → cyanoacrylate (glue) injection
balloon tamponade — Sengstaken–Blakemore / Minnesota tube (bridge, ≤24 h; risks: aspiration, mucosal pressure necrosis,
migration) OR self-expanding covered oesophageal stent (Danis)
TIPS for uncontrolled or rebleeding despite endoscopic + drug therapy; pre-emptive (“early”) TIPS within 72 h in high-risk
(Child–Pugh C <14, or Child B with active bleeding at OGD) improves survival (García-Pagán, NEJM 2010)
NSBB — carvedilol (Baveno choice) + EVL programme to variceal eradication; TIPS if further bleeding
protect airway (aspiration risk); avoid over-transfusion (target Hb 70 — raises portal pressure / rebleed); treat
Early Hb can be normal (haemoconcentration) — a normal Hb does not exclude major bleeding.
Raised urea : creatinine ratio points to an UPPER GI source (absorbed digested blood).
PPI before endoscopy is NOT recommended (NICE) and never replaces endoscopic haemostasis.
Adrenaline injection alone is inadequate — always add a second modality.
Aortoenteric fistula: suspect in any UGIB with prior aortic graft / AAA repair (herald bleed → catastrophic).
Known chronic liver disease → give terlipressin + antibiotics empirically; don’t wait for endoscopy.
Sources: NICE CG141 (2016) · BSG acute UGIB & variceal guidance · Baveno VII consensus (2022). Scores: Glasgow–Blatchford, Rockall, AIMS65.
Key evidence: Forrest (Lancet 1974) · Laine & Peterson (NEJM 1994) rebleed rates · Villanueva (NEJM 2013) restrictive transfusion · García-Pagán (NEJM 2010) early TIPS.
Revision aid — verify against current guidance before clinical use. Rebleed rates are natural-history estimates and vary with treatment.
Revision resources & realistic mock exams · mockfrcs.co.uk
